title: Scottish treacle scones
subtitle: Spiced scones darkened with black treacle
excerpt: Scottish treacle scones are a dark, spiced scone made with black treacle stirred into the buttermilk. The treacle adds a deep, slightly bitter flavour and a dark colour that contrasts with the pale interior. They are a traditional scone of Scotland, particularly associated with Hallowe'en, and are always spiced with mixed spice and ginger.
sourceNotes: Treacle scones are a traditional Scottish bake documented in Scottish domestic cookery. F. Marian McNeill, The Scots Kitchen (1929), documents treacle scones as a Scottish standard, particularly associated with Hallowe'en festivities. The form here follows the traditional formula: treacle, mixed spice, ginger, buttermilk.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked; reheat from frozen in a 160°C oven for 12 minutes.

content.text: Treacle scones are made by warming black treacle slightly and stirring it into the buttermilk before mixing it into the flour. The treacle colours the dough dark and carries through into the baked scone as a deep, slightly smoky flavour that the spices back up. They rise the same as any scone but look entirely different from the outside: deep mahogany brown on top, pale at the split.

items.prepNote: warmed slightly to make it pourable

content.text: Sift the flour, baking powder, mixed spice, and ginger into a large bowl. Rub in the cold butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in the sugar. Stir the warmed treacle into the buttermilk until dissolved. Add to the flour and mix with a palette knife in 8 to 10 strokes until just combined. The dough will be dark and slightly sticky.

Heat the oven to 220°C fan. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and pat to 3 cm thick. Cut rounds with a floured 5 cm cutter, pressing straight down without twisting. Place on a lined baking tray and brush the tops with milk. Bake for 13 to 15 minutes. The colour is deep already from the treacle; check doneness by pressing the top (it should feel firm) or by tapping the base for a hollow sound. Cool briefly and eat warm with butter.
